name: Los Huertos - Tamajón – circular a las ruinas del Monasterio Bonaval
region_intro: Mountain biking around Retiendas features a landscape characterized by varied terrain, including areas with historical ruins and natural formations. The region offers a mix of challenging climbs and more gentle paths, with routes often traversing through rural settings and near small villages. Elevation gains can be significant on some trails, providing panoramic views of the surrounding countryside. The area's geology and vegetation contribute to diverse riding experiences for mountain bikers. Best mountain bike trails around Retiendas The most popular mountain bike trail is Los Huertos - Tamajón – circular a las ruinas del Monasterio Bonaval, an easy 13.6 miles (21.9 km) trail that takes 1 hour 51 minutes to complete. This route offers a circular path around historical ruins. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Bonaval Monastery Ruins – Roman Bridge of Valdesotos loop from Tamajón, a difficult 31.1 miles (49.9 km) path. This route features significant elevation changes and passes by both monastery ruins and a Roman bridge. Local mountain bikers also love the Matarrubia – Iglesia de Matarrubia loop from Puebla de Valles, a 18.5 miles (29.7 km) trail leading through rural landscapes, often completed in about 2 hours 30 minutes. Mountain biking around Retiendas is defined by historical sites, varied elevation profiles, and natural landscapes. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy loops to more challenging long-distance rides. The routes in Retiendas are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.75 stars from over 8 reviews. More than 180 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Retiendas's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Retiendas?
A: There are over 18 mountain bike trails documented around Retiendas on komoot, offering a variety of experiences for different skill levels.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Retiendas?
A: Retiendas experiences a Mediterranean climate with hot, dry summers and mild winters. Spring (April-June) and Autumn (September-November) generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for mountain biking, avoiding the intense summer heat and potential winter chill or rain. However, trails are accessible year-round, with conditions varying.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for beginners in Retiendas?
A: Yes, Retiendas offers several easy trails. An excellent option for beginners is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1006154663/los-huertos-tamajon-circular-a-las-ruinas-del-monasterio-bonaval" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Huertos - Tamajón – circular a las ruinas del Monasterio Bonaval&lt;/a&gt;, an easy 13.6-mile (21.9 km) circular route that takes about 1 hour 51 minutes to complete and passes historical ruins.

Q: What are some challenging mountain bike trails for experienced riders?
A: For experienced riders seeking a challenge, the region offers several difficult routes. Consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/17508088" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bonaval Monastery Ruins – Roman Bridge of Valdesotos loop from Tamajón&lt;/a&gt;, a difficult 31.1-mile (49.9 km) path with significant elevation changes. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19812709" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Great Wall Bridge – Campillejo loop from Tamajón&lt;/a&gt;, which spans over 80 km and features substantial climbs.

Q: Are there family-friendly mountain bike routes in Retiendas?
A: While the region has varied terrain, some easier trails can be suitable for families with older children or those comfortable with longer distances. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1006154663/los-huertos-tamajon-circular-a-las-ruinas-del-monasterio-bonaval" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Huertos - Tamajón – circular a las ruinas del Monasterio Bonaval&lt;/a&gt; route is an easy, circular option that might be suitable for a family outing.

Q: What kind of natural and historical sights can I see along the mountain bike trails?
A: The trails around Retiendas often traverse areas rich in history and natural beauty. You can encounter historical ruins, such as those along the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1006154663/los-huertos-tamajon-circular-a-las-ruinas-del-monasterio-bonaval" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Huertos - Tamajón – circular a las ruinas del Monasterio Bonaval&lt;/a&gt; route, and the Roman Bridge of Valdesotos on the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/17508088" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bonaval Monastery Ruins – Roman Bridge of Valdesotos loop&lt;/a&gt;. The region also features natural formations and rural landscapes, with highlights like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5430967" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Trillos Bridge&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5323339" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of Los Enebrales&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there many circular mountain bike routes in Retiendas?
A: Yes, many of the mountain bike routes in Retiendas are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish at the same point. Examples include the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1006154663/los-huertos-tamajon-circular-a-las-ruinas-del-monasterio-bonaval" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Huertos - Tamajón – circular a las ruinas del Monasterio Bonaval&lt;/a&gt; and the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10417198" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Enchanted City of Tamajón – Hermitage of Los Enebrales loop from Tamajón&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about the trails in Retiendas?
A: The mountain bike trails around Retiendas are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.75 stars from over 8 reviews. Riders often praise the diverse terrain, the blend of historical sites with natural landscapes, and the options available for various skill levels, from easy loops to challenging long-distance rides.

Q: Where can I find parking for mountain biking trails in Retiendas?
A: Many routes around Retiendas start from villages like Tamajón or Puebla de Valles, where you can typically find public parking. It's advisable to check the specific route details on komoot for recommended starting points and parking information, especially for less-trafficked trailheads.

Q: Are there any lakes or reservoirs accessible by mountain bike in the Retiendas area?
A: Yes, the region around Retiendas features several bodies of water. You can find highlights such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2699850" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Views of the reservoir from the Royuelo ravine&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7464158" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lagunas de La Puebla de Beleña&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6619443" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vado Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;, which can be incorporated into or viewed from various mountain bike routes.

Q: What kind of wildlife might I encounter on the mountain bike trails?
A: The rural landscapes and natural areas around Retiendas are home to various wildlife. Depending on the season and time of day, you might spot birds of prey, deer, wild boar, or smaller mammals. Always ride responsibly and respect the natural habitat.

Q: Are there options for less crowded mountain bike trails in Retiendas?
A: While some popular routes can see more traffic, the extensive network of trails around Retiendas means you can often find quieter paths. Exploring some of the moderate or difficult routes, or riding during off-peak hours (early mornings or weekdays), can help you discover more secluded sections of the region.
tips: The Tamajon lagoon has a nice picnic area and a small shelter with a roof, perfect for overnight stays.

The remains that currently survive date from the 13th century. Various reforms were made in the 17th century and it was finally abandoned by the monks in 1821, passing into private hands. It was declared an Asset of Cultural Interest in 1992, being restored and conditioned for visits by the Castilla-La Mancha Community Board in 2018.
Of the monastic complex, the apse, the sacristy, the transept and the southern nave of the church are preserved, as well as some walls of other dependencies.
